skill.name: frontend-design
skill.description: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, artifacts, posters, or applications (examples include websites, landing pages, dashboards, React components, HTML/CSS layouts, or when styling/beautifying any web UI). Generates creative, polished code and UI design that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
skill.summary: Guide AI agents to build polished, distinctive web interfaces with production-ready frontend design choices.
skill.author: Azeem-2

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported network, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found prose-only false positives. The skill contains frontend design guidance and an Apache license, with no executable code, external command use, credential access, or prompt injection attempt.

low_findings.title: Static Network Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL is the standard Apache License homepage in license text. No code performs a network request or sends data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The location is license prose containing the Apache project URL. There is no executable network API or data transfer path.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts are substring matches inside normal license and design prose. No hashing, encryption, cipher selection, or authentication logic exists.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are markdown text and Apache license clauses. I found no cryptographic API calls, algorithm configuration, or code execution surface.

low_findings.title: Static Reconnaissance Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts occur in design guidance about aesthetics, typography, and visual backgrounds. No commands inspect the host, filesystem, network, users, or environment.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The content is descriptive frontend design instruction. There are no shell commands, filesystem probes, or platform discovery steps.

content.value_statement: Generic web interfaces make products feel unfinished and forgettable. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward polished frontend work with clear visual direction.
actual_capabilities: Guides frontend builds toward a clear visual concept before implementation.
Supports pages, components, dashboards, posters, artifacts, and web applications.
Encourages production-ready HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React, Vue, or similar frontend code.
Provides design direction for typography, color, motion, layout, and visual detail.
Helps avoid common AI-generated interface patterns and generic styling choices.
limitations: Does not include executable scripts, templates, or reusable component files.
Does not perform accessibility, browser, or performance testing by itself.
Depends on the host AI agent and project tools for actual implementation.
May need stronger product constraints for regulated or brand-sensitive work.

use_cases.title: Launch a Product Page
use_cases.description: Create a memorable landing page with stronger typography, layout, color, and motion choices.

use_cases.title: Refine an Internal Dashboard
use_cases.description: Improve an existing tool so data, actions, and hierarchy are easier to scan.

use_cases.title: Build a Polished Component
use_cases.description: Turn a rough component request into frontend code with a consistent visual point of view.

prompt_templates.title: Improve a Simple Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-design skill to redesign this page. Keep the content, but improve layout, typography, color, and interaction polish.

prompt_templates.title: Create a New Component
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-design skill to build a production-ready component for this use case. Choose a distinct visual direction and implement it.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Full App View
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-design skill to create a complete app screen. Include navigation, states, controls, responsive layout, and visual hierarchy.

prompt_templates.title: Rework a Design System Surface
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-design skill to redesign this interface within our brand constraints. Preserve function while improving density, rhythm, motion, and accessibility.

output_examples.input: Build a dashboard for a subscription analytics product.
output_examples.output: A focused dashboard concept with clear metrics, dense tables, restrained motion, strong hierarchy, and production-ready frontend structure.

output_examples.input: Make this landing page feel less generic.
output_examples.output: A redesigned page with a stronger visual concept, distinctive typography, richer spacing, sharper calls to action, and responsive polish.

output_examples.input: Create a settings panel for a collaborative editor.
output_examples.output: A practical panel design with grouped controls, compact spacing, clear states, accessible labels, and subtle interface feedback.

best_practices: Provide audience, product context, brand constraints, and required technology before asking for implementation.
Ask for working frontend code, responsive behavior, and key UI states in the same request.
Review generated designs for accessibility, performance, and consistency with your existing product.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for only a prettier version without explaining the product goal.
Do not accept visual effects that reduce readability, responsiveness, or task completion.
Do not use this skill as a replacement for user research or brand approval.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create polished frontend interfaces, including pages, components, dashboards, artifacts, posters, and web applications.

faq.question: Does it generate working code?
faq.answer: It guides the AI agent to produce working frontend code when the host environment supports implementation.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is it tied to one framework?
faq.answer: No. The instructions mention HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React, Vue, and similar frontend approaches.

faq.question: Does it include security-sensitive behavior?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The reviewed files contain markdown guidance and license text, not executable code.

faq.question: How should I get the best result?
faq.answer: Give a clear use case, audience, constraints, and required states before asking for the interface.
